info.name: Retrieve Historical Audio Recordings and Related Metadata Within Factset Coverage.

params.name: _paginationLimit
params.description: Specifies the number of results to return per page. [ Min=0 ; Max=500 ]

params.name: _paginationOffset
params.description: Specifies the starting point for pagination. This parameter is used to identify the beginning of next set of results.

params.name: year
params.description: Specifies the year for which the historical audio recordings and related metadata are to be retrieved.

params.name: trimmed
params.description: Specifies if trimmed/untrimmed historical audio recordings should be returned.

items.docs:
* Returns the **untrimmed** historical audio recordings and related metadata dating back from May 10, 2011 to Sep 30, 2022.

* Returns the **trimmed** historical audio recordings and related metadata dating back from May 10, 2011 to Dec 31, 2022.



Query parameters can be used to filter and narrow down the results.

info.name: Retrieve Latest Audio Recordings and Related Metadata Within Factset Coverage.

params.name: _sort
params.description: Sorts results in chronological order, reverse chronological order and by uploadTime(latest uploaded first). Results are in reverse chronological order by default.

params.name: _paginationLimit
params.description: Specifies the number of results to return per page. [ Min=0 ; Max=500 ]

params.name: startDate
params.description: Used in conjuction with endDate. The earliest date of the audio file the API should fetch for (can be in absolute: **YYYY-MM-DD** or relative date: **-1** for yesterday).

params.name: _paginationOffset
params.description: Specifies the starting point for pagination. This parameter is used to identify the beginning of next set of results.

params.name: endDate
params.description: Used in conjuction with startDate. The latest date of the audio file the API should fetch for (can be in absolute: **YYYY-MM-DD** or relative date: **0** for today).

params.name: reportId
params.description: Unique identifier for fetching the audio file for an event. The same ID is used for the transcript of the same event.

params.name: audioSourceId
params.description: Unique ID for an Internal recording specific to reportID. For example, ReportID X would have multiple recordings from different source (phone or webcast or vendor or replay). One ReportID can have multiple AudioSourceIDs.

params.name: ids
params.description: This parameter filters the results based on ticker-region or Entity ID or the combination of both. A comma is used to separate each identifier.

params.name: sourceCode
params.description:
This parameter filters the results based on Source of the Audio file. Below are the descriptions for each Source Code -
* P = Phone
* W = Webcast
* V = Vendor
* I = Webcast Replay
* F = Flash - identical to webcast; can merge with "W" in the future
* R = Replay (Phone Replay)

params.name: fileName
params.description: This parameter is used to filter the data on based on the file name.

params.name: trimmed
params.description: This parameters helps to search trimmed audio files.

params.name: uploadTime
params.description:
This parameter filters data based on uploadTime relative to the current time, in hours. For example:- uploadTime = -15 (fetches audio files between 15 hours ago and now)

Minimum is 1 hour i.e., uploadTime= -1

Maximum is 1 week/168 hours i.e., uploadTime=-168

While using uploadTime, the startDate and endDate parameters will be ignored.

items.docs: Returns the latest audio recordings. Query parameters can be used to filter and narrow down the results.

info.name: Factset Returns the Active Calls Happening at the Moment.

params.name: _paginationLimit
params.description: Specifies the  number of results to return per page. [ Min=0; Max=500]

params.name: _paginationOffset
params.description: Specifies the starting point for pagination. This parameter is used to identify the beginning of next set of results.

params.name: audioSourceId
params.description: Unique ID for an Internal recording specific to reportID. For example, ReportID X would have multiple recordings from different source (dial-in or webcast). One ReportID can have multiple AudioSourceIDs.

params.name: reportId
params.description: Unique identifier for an event.

params.name: _sort
params.description: Enables to get the data in chronological or reverse chronological order based on startDate. Results are in reverse chronological order if this parameter is not used.

params.name: entityId
params.description: Factset entity level identifier for the company hosting the event.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Ticker-region identifier for the company hosting the event.

params.name: callStatus
params.description: Status of the call i.e. ended or inProgress or ewn or issueAtSource.

items.docs: Returns the active calls happening at the moment

info.name: Factset Returns the Latest Transcript Snippets From an Active Call.

params.name: _paginationLimit
params.description: Specifies the  number of results to return per page. [ Min=0; Max=500]

params.name: _paginationOffset
params.description: Specifies the starting point for pagination. This parameter is used to identify the beginning of next set of results.

params.name: audioSourceId
params.description: Unique ID for an Internal recording specific to reportID. For example, ReportID X would have multiple recordings from different source (dial-in or webcast). One ReportID can have multiple AudioSourceIDs.

params.name: reportId
params.description: Unique identifier for an event.

items.docs: Returns the latest snippets from an active call

info.name: Factset Returns the Latest Speakerids With the Confidence Scores Generated for an Active Call.

params.name: _paginationLimit
params.description: Specifies the  number of results to return per page. [ Min=0; Max=500]

params.name: _paginationOffset
params.description: Specifies the starting point for pagination. This parameter is used to identify the beginning of next set of results.

params.name: audioSourceId
params.description: Unique ID for an Internal recording specific to reportID. For example, ReportID X would have multiple recordings from different source (dial-in or webcast). One ReportID can have multiple AudioSourceIDs.

params.name: _sort
params.description: Enables to get the data in chronological or reverse chronological order. Results are in chronological order if this parameter is not used.

items.docs: Returns the latest speakerIds with the cosine scores(confidence scores) generated for an active call.

info.name: Factset Returns the Indexed Transcript Data in Small Increments Throughout the Duration of an Active Call.

params.name: _paginationLimit
params.description: Specifies the  number of results to return per page. [ Min=0; Max=50 ]

params.name: _paginationOffset
params.description: Specifies the starting point for pagination. This parameter is used to identify the beginning of next set of results.

params.name: audioSourceId
params.description: Unique ID for an Internal recording specific to reportID. For example, ReportID X would have multiple recordings from different source (dial-in or webcast). One ReportID can have multiple AudioSourceIDs.

params.name: reportId
params.description: Unique identifier for an event.

items.docs: Returns the  indexed transcript data  in small increments throughout the duration of an active call.
